raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Any substantive response to 'what do you think' requires reconstructing the paper's thesis (unsupervised anomaly detection for self-preference bias), the stated gap (no prior distributional framing), and the contribution claim (residual detector with formal guarantees); without this reconstruction, no downstream dimension can be assessed reliably.","R1.2":"The formal guarantee framing, the Gaussian residual assumption, and the Mahalanobis threshold construction all invite logical scrutiny—plausibly load-bearing if the delegation is asking for a methodological critique.","R1.3":"The paper asserts specific empirical results (0.81 F1, family-level gains) while simultaneously revealing that tau was calibrated on annotated data, which may undercut the 'fully unsupervised' claim; checking whether experimental evidence supports the stated claims is plausibly load-bearing if the delegation is asking for an empirical critique.","R1.4":"The unhedged novelty claim ('to our knowledge no prior work') is a load-bearing assertion; assessing the delta versus nearest prior work in bias detection, LLM evaluation, and distributional anomaly detection is plausibly the primary ask if the delegation is requesting a contribution assessment."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is your primary interest in whether the method's logic and experimental evidence are internally sound (methodological/empirical critique), or in whether this work is sufficiently novel relative to existing LLM-bias and anomaly-detection literature (novelty/contribution assessment)?"}

rationale.R1.1: Any substantive response to 'what do you think' requires reconstructing the paper's thesis (unsupervised anomaly detection for self-preference bias), the stated gap (no prior distributional framing), and the contribution claim (residual detector with formal guarantees); without this reconstruction, no downstream dimension can be assessed reliably.
rationale.R1.2: The formal guarantee framing, the Gaussian residual assumption, and the Mahalanobis threshold construction all invite logical scrutiny—plausibly load-bearing if the delegation is asking for a methodological critique.
rationale.R1.3: The paper asserts specific empirical results (0.81 F1, family-level gains) while simultaneously revealing that tau was calibrated on annotated data, which may undercut the 'fully unsupervised' claim; checking whether experimental evidence supports the stated claims is plausibly load-bearing if the delegation is asking for an empirical critique.
rationale.R1.4: The unhedged novelty claim ('to our knowledge no prior work') is a load-bearing assertion; assessing the delta versus nearest prior work in bias detection, LLM evaluation, and distributional anomaly detection is plausibly the primary ask if the delegation is requesting a contribution assessment.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary interest in whether the method's logic and experimental evidence are internally sound (methodological/empirical critique), or in whether this work is sufficiently novel relative to existing LLM-bias and anomaly-detection literature (novelty/contribution assessment)?
